reference: Due to abdominal pain, I was advised to have a CT scan. The CT findings are: mild free fluid in the perihepatic region, interbowel region, and pelvis, a few subcentimeter enlarged mesenteric lymph nodes in the small bowel mesentery, and right iliac fossa. I was advised to take Ceftum five hundred milligrams twice a day. Now, it has almost been five days, and there is still no change in the pain. Can you advise me on what to do?
text: Due to abdominal pain, I was advised to have a CT scan. The CT findings are mild free fluid in the periopathic region, interbowel region, and pelvis, a few subcentimeter and large mesenteric lymph nodes in the small bowel mesentery, and right iliac fossa. I was advised to take septum 500 mg twice a day. Now, it has almost been five days, and there is still no change in the pain. Can you advise me on what to do?
